title: strychnos benthamii
intro: Strychnos benthamii, a plant species in the family Loganiaceae, is a perennial vine native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Strychnos benthamii are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a digestive aid and anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of Strychnos benthamii are typically made by infusing the leaves and roots in water or alcohol to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties.
